LHX.sentence: L3Harris is down about 1% but sitting at an 18% drawdown from its highs, which looks like a decent setup for a defense contractor that's been trading cheaper than its bigger peers.

ASML.sentence: ASML dropped a bit today but is still running about 22% above its recent average — normal behavior for the only company that makes the machines needed for advanced chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde had a quiet up day, continuing its steady grind higher as the world's biggest industrial gas supplier keeps benefiting from long-term contracts and pricing power.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down nearly 30% from its highs, which is unusual for a company with such strong profit margins — the AI infrastructure story is intact but the stock got ahead of itself.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher looks cheap right now, trading near the low end of its normal range despite being the essential supplier of lab equipment to the entire biotech industry.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady, up slightly, though its dividend yield is getting close to what you can earn on government bonds, which reduces its appeal as an income play.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow didn't have data today — the enterprise AI automation story continues without any major updates.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore jumped over 5% as its diversified mining and commodity trading business is getting credit for being more than just a pure copper play.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is up slightly but still sitting about 27% below its recent highs, which historically has been a good accumulation zone for patient investors.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA rose about 3% with the Mexican peso holding steady — currency moves are the main thing that matters for this Latin American bottler.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is down about 1% despite oil prices staying well above what the company needs to be profitable — investors are worried these might be peak earnings for the cycle.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk rose 2% and is trading below book value, suggesting the container shipping giant's fleet is priced near replacement cost while earnings look sustainable rather than cyclically high.
